heroHeadline: Iran Ceasefire Deadline Day: Trump Extends Terms, Maintains Blockade — 8 Days to Vancouver FIFA Congress With 50 Days to Kickoff
heroSubtitle: The two-week US-Iran ceasefire brokered April 8 reached its deadline on April 22 with Trump extending the terms but maintaining a US naval blockade of Iranian ports — which Iran called an 'act of war' and refused to negotiate under. The extension moves the window past the April 30 FIFA Congress in Vancouver (8 days away), the formal decision point for Iran's World Cup participation, without resolving the core security dispute. Iran's Sports Minister Ahmad Donyamali said April 21 that 'maybe the decision is not to go,' with the Supreme National Security Council holding final authority. Separately, FIFA released a new batch of tickets for all 104 matches at 11 AM ET on April 22 — exactly 50 days before the June 11 opener — with the $60 Supporter Entry Tier available in a first-come, first-served drop, as more than 5 million tickets have already been sold.
footerNote: Data sourced from FIFA.com, UEFA.com, US Soccer, CONCACAF, ESPN, The Athletic, BBC Sport, AP, Reuters, NPR, Al Jazeera, Human Rights Watch, Amnesty International, American Immigration Council, Middle East Monitor, CBS Sports, Bloomberg, Fortune, UPI, CNN, CNBC, NBC Dallas-Fort Worth, Fox 5 New York, Inside World Football, CBC Sports, Johnson County Post, PR Newswire, Yahoo Sports
